title: Wireless Hacking 📶
description: How WiFi can be intercepted and cracked.

slides.title: Airborne Attacks 📶
slides.content:
### Hacking Thin Air

WiFi signals travel through walls. Anyone with a $20 wireless adapter can 'listen' to your data if it isn't encrypted properly.

slides.title: Encryption Standards 🔑
slides.content:
### The Good, The Bad, The Safe

1.  **WEP**: Old, weak, and can be cracked in seconds.
2.  **WPA2**: The current standard. Safe, but vulnerable to 'Handshake' capture.
3.  **WPA3**: The newest and most secure standard.

slides.title: Beware Public WiFi 🛑
slides.content:
### The Evil Twin

An attacker can set up a fake hotspot called `Free_Starbucks_WiFi`. When you connect, they intercept (Man-in-the-Middle) everything you do online. **ALWAYS use a VPN on public WiFi!**

quizData.question: Which wireless encryption standard is considered obsolete and highly insecure?
quizData.explanation: WEP (Wired Equivalent Privacy) was broken years ago and should never be used.
